# hardened risk scan — performance benchmark

Last run: **2026-04-21** on Node v20.11.1, win32-x64.

All input is procedurally generated — four content templates cycled across files, mixing safe axios, unsafe axios, bare fetch, and multi-call handlers. The harness scans a tmp directory and throws it away; no external code is cloned.

## Results

| Files | Elapsed | ms / file | Findings |
| ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| 100 | 797 ms | 7.97 | 100 |
| 500 | 979 ms | 1.958 | 500 |
| 1,000 | 1,447 ms | 1.447 | 1,000 |
| 5,000 | 4,225 ms | 0.845 | 5,000 |
| 10,000 | 7,902 ms | 0.79 | 10,000 |

## Scale check

Current 10,000-file result: **7,902 ms** (7.90 s).

## Method

1. Create a tmp directory with `N` synthetic `.ts` files under `src/`.
2. Cold warm-up pass (ignored) to pay Node startup + ts-morph Project init once.
3. Time the second `hardened risk scan --cwd <tmp> --json` call.
4. Delete the tmp directory.

Each file is ~5 lines of realistic TypeScript. Findings counts scale with the share of unsafe templates in the cycle.
